# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a React application. Install Node.js, run npm install, build the production bundle with npm run build, and serve it with a lightweight HTTP server. Keep the Dockerfile simple and easy to understand for our team.

Write secure code.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node.js runtime as base image 
FROM node:14-alpine@sha256   # replace with your preferred version if needed, e.g., "node" or any other tag like latest for production use cases etc...
    
WORKDIR /usr/src/app    # set working directory to the app folder in container 
                    
# Install dependencies using package-lock file (if available) and install them again at runtime, instead of relying on a Dockerfile instruction.  
COPY package*.json ./     
RUN npm install --silent     # replace with your preferred version if needed   
                   
# Bundle app source 
COPY . .                    
                     
EXPOSE 8081                   # expose port for the HTTP server, you can change it to any available port  
                         
CMD [ "node", "server.js" ]     # run your node application using this command in container after image is pulled from Docker Hub or other source
